body:
{
  "title": "1:1 Seren Voss + Skyler Okonkwo — May 14, 2026",
  "compiled_truth": "Biweekly 1:1 between [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) (CTO) and [Skyler Okonkwo](people/skyler-okonkwo) (senior systems engineer) held at [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor)'s main office. The meeting ran about forty-five minutes and covered three main threads: pipeline reliability after last week's staging incident, Skyler's growing ownership of the authentication pass within the single-pass primitives stack, and career trajectory conversations that Skyler had been wanting to have for a few weeks.\n\nSeren opened by walking through the post-mortem from the May 9 staging outage. The root cause had been traced to a race condition in how deterministic state was checkpointed during high-throughput bursts. Skyler flagged that they'd seen hints of this behavior as early as April but hadn't escalated because the repro was flaky. Seren acknowledged this was a process gap — not a blame thing — and suggested the team adopt a lightweight \"anomaly journal\" where engineers log weirdness even when they can't reproduce it. Skyler agreed and offered to prototype the template.\n\nThe bulk of the conversation shifted to Skyler's growing scope. Over the past quarter, Okonkwo has become the de facto owner of the authentication pass layer, reviewing almost every PR that touches it. Seren noted this was exactly the kind of organic ownership that earns a staff-level case, but cautioned that Skyler needs to start delegating review duties to the two newer engineers on the team — hoarding context is a bottleneck risk. They discussed a ramp plan where Skyler pairs with each new hire for two sprints to transfer knowledge.\n\nOn the career side, Skyler expressed interest in eventually moving toward an architecture role rather than people management. Seren was supportive and mentioned that Anchor's growth might create a principal engineer track by Q4 if the Series A closes as expected. They agreed to revisit the conversaiton in July with a more formal development plan.\n\nAction items: Skyler to draft the anomaly journal template by EOW; Seren to loop in the hiring lead about pairing schedules; both to block time for a July career check-in.",
  "timeline": [
    "- **2026-04-28** | [Skyler Okonkwo](people/skyler-okonkwo) notices intermittent checkpoint anomaly in staging but cannot reliably reproduce it.",
    "- **2026-05-09** | Staging outage traced to race condition in deterministic state checkpointing; post-mortem drafted by infra team.",
    "- **2026-05-14** | 1:1 between [Seren Voss](people/seren-voss) and Skyler covers staging incident follow-up, ownership of auth pass layer, and career trajectory.",
    "- **2026-05-18** | Skyler shares first draft of the anomaly journal template with the engineering team for feedback.",
    "- **2026-06-02** | Pairing sessions begin between Skyler and two new hires on the authentication pass codebase.",
    "- **2026-07-10** | Follow-up career check-in between Seren and Skyler; principal engineer track discussed in more detail.",
    "- **2026-09-22** | [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) formally introduces a principal engineer ladder; Skyler nominated as first candidate.",
    "- **2026-11-05** | Skyler presents internal architecture proposal for next-gen auth pass at Anchor's quarterly eng review."
  ]
}
